description:
A compliance officer has asked you to demonstrate that the current terminal
    session is explicitly configured to use the UTC time-zone and the
    en_US.UTF-8 locale.  
    To satisfy the officer’s documentation requirements, do the following
    in your terminal session:

    1. Create a directory named
       /home/user/compliance_audit  
       (make sure the directory actually exists at that exact path).

    2. Inside that directory create a shell script called
       /home/user/compliance_audit/enforce_time_locale.sh  
       The script must be a valid POSIX‐compatible bash script that contains
       *exactly and only* the following four lines (including the shebang on
       the first line and the terminating newline at the end of the last
       line):

            #!/bin/bash
            export TZ=UTC
            export LANG=en_US.UTF-8
            export LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8

       • There must be no blank lines before, between, or after those lines.  
       • The file must be marked executable.

    3. After you have **executed** the script in the current shell so that the
       variables are in effect, create a plain-text log file called

           /home/user/compliance_audit/time_locale_report.txt

       The log file must contain *exactly three lines* in the precise order
       shown below, each terminated with a single newline character and **no
       additional whitespace** anywhere on any line:

           Timezone=UTC
           Locale=en_US.UTF-8
           Audit_Result=PASS

       The automated test suite will read the file byte-for-byte, so the
       spelling, capitalization, ordering, and lack of extra spaces are all
       mandatory.

    4. When you are done, you should be able to run the following two commands
       in the same shell and see the expected values (this is for your own
       verification; the grading script only checks the file and script
       contents):

           echo "$TZ"
           echo "$LANG"

       Both should output “UTC” and “en_US.UTF-8” respectively.

    Summary of artefacts that **must exist** when you finish:

    • Directory: /home/user/compliance_audit  
    • Executable script with the exact 4-line content shown above:
      /home/user/compliance_audit/enforce_time_locale.sh  
    • Log file with the exact 3-line content shown above:
      /home/user/compliance_audit/time_locale_report.txt

    No other files are required.  Do not include timestamps, comments, or
    additional text anywhere.  Once these items are in place, the compliance
    officer can sign off the audit.
truth:
Directory that must exist:
        /home/user/compliance_audit

    Script that must exist:
        /home/user/compliance_audit/enforce_time_locale.sh
    Exact byte-for-byte content (including trailing newline):
        "#!/bin/bash\nexport TZ=UTC\nexport LANG=en_US.UTF-8\nexport LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8\n"

    Report file that must exist:
        /home/user/compliance_audit/time_locale_report.txt
    Exact byte-for-byte content (including trailing newline):
        "Timezone=UTC\nLocale=en_US.UTF-8\nAudit_Result=PASS\n"

    File permissions:
        enforce_time_locale.sh must have executable bit set (e.g., 755 or 750).
        The report file can be 644.

    No other files, sub-directories, or content are evaluated by the grader.
